Cross Hall of the White House on Wednesday, April 1, 2026, in Washington.     Alex Brandon/Pool via REUTERS <a data-testid="Link" target="_blank" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reutersconnect.com/item/us-president-donald-trump-delivers-an-address-to-the-nation-about-the-iran-war-at-the-white-house-in-washington/dGFnOnJldXRlcnMuY29tLDIwMjY6bmV3c21sX1JDMlBHS0E2OUEwWQ%3D%3D/?utm_medium=rcom-article-media&utm_campaign=rcom-rcp-lead" rel="noopener" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC link-module__with-icon__qlg76 collapsible-caption-module__license__CPQvV">Purchase Licensing<span><span class="link-module__suffix__ObiNY"> Rights</span><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 32 32" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" role="presentation" data-testid="NewTabSymbol" class="link-module__new-tab-symbol__d53s7 link-module__move-left__CCZH6 collapsible-caption-module__symbol__943UJ" fill="#666" width="0" height="0"><path fill="#666" d="M15 6c.6 0 1 .4 1 1s-.4 1-1 1H9c-.6 0-1 .4-1 1v14a1.1 1.1 0 0 0 .2.6c.1.2.4.4.8.4h14c.6 0 1-.4 1-1v-6c0-.6.4-1 1-1s1 .4 1 1v6a3 3 0 0 1-3 3H9a3 3 0 0 1-2.2-.9A3 3 0 0 1 6 23V9a3 3 0 0 1 3-3Zm10 0 .4.1.5.5.1.4v6c0 .6-.4 1-1 1s-1-.4-1-1V9.4l-5.3 5.3a1 1 0 0 1-.7.3 1 1 0 0 1-.7-.3 1 1 0 0 1 0-1.4L22.6 8H19c-.6 0-1-.4-1-1s.4-1 1-1Z"></path></svg><span style="border:0;clip:rect(0 0 0 0);clip-path:inset(50%);height:1px;margin:-1px;overflow:hidden;padding:0;position:absolute;width:1px;white-space:nowrap">, opens new tab</span></span></a></span></div></figure></div><div class="article-body-module__content__bnXL1"><div data-testid="paragraph-0" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">April 2 - Donald Trump’s <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-tell-wary-public-that-iran-war-goals-have-been-accomplished-prime-time-2026-04-01/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">speech to Americans</a> on Wednesday told us little new about a month-old war that’s come to dominate his presidency. But the U.S. president’s comments this week to allies and even reporters about the limits on his power at home and abroad have been more revealing.</div><div data-testid="paragraph-1" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">Over the weekend, demonstrators opposed to Trump’s policies <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/us/rallies-planned-thousands-us-cities-no-kings-protest-against-trump-2026-03-28/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">gathered ​in thousands of U.S. cities</a> for protests held under the banner of “No Kings.”</div><div data-testid="element" class="article-body-module__element__5eCce"></div><div data-testid="paragraph-2" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">"They call me king now, do you believe it? ‘No king,’” Trump said ‌at the White House on Wednesday. "I'm such a king I can't get <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/us-judge-halts-trumps-400-million-white-house-ballroom-project-now-2026-03-31/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">a ballroom</a> approved… I could be doing a lot more if I was a king.”</div><div data-testid="paragraph-3" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">Candid, lively and discursive, those were Trump’s unfiltered remarks to close allies gathered for an Easter lunch. In the remarks, a powerful president who often projects confidence reflected on the restraints he faces. The comments came after Trump visited the Supreme Court in a ​historic first for a president and heard justices he appointed skeptically question a lawyer defending <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/legal/government/us-supreme-court-considers-trumps-effort-limit-birthright-citizenship-2026-04-01/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">his limits on birthright citizenship</a>. (Trump was accompanied to the court by Attorney ​General Pam Bondi, who he fired on Thursday)</div><div data-testid="element" class="article-body-module__element__5eCce"></div><div data-testid="paragraph-4" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">Trump also suggested the financial demands of pursuing the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran were forcing ⁠hard choices at home. The federal government could not, he said, focus on issues like the cost of childcare. “We can't take care of daycare," Trump said. "We have to take ​care of one thing: military protection. We have to guard the country."</div><div data-testid="paragraph-5" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">Those comments were not what the public heard from the president on Wednesday. The event was closed to ​me and the other members of the press pool covering Trump that day and only became public when the White House briefly posted a video of the remarks online - and then deleted them shortly thereafter.</div><div data-testid="element" class="article-body-module__element__5eCce"></div><div data-testid="paragraph-6" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">What the public saw instead Wednesday night was a different Trump: serious, defiant and tightly scripted, <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/us/takeaways-trumps-speech-iran-2026-04-02/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">delivering a primetime address</a> that did little to assuage fears that the Middle East conflict could widen.</div><div data-testid="paragraph-7" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">The contrast was ​stark. When <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/us-leave-iran-pretty-quickly-return-if-needed-trump-tells-reuters-2026-04-01/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">my colleague Steve Holland</a> called Trump before the speech, the president vented about NATO in aggrieved language that never appeared in the formal address.</div><div data-testid="paragraph-8" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">As reporters who follow Trump, ​we track the president’s moods and rhetorical styles closely. Teleprompter Trump is different from the looser telephone Trump, and both are different from the president at a rally or among his closest ‌confidants.</div><div data-testid="element" class="article-body-module__element__5eCce"></div><div data-testid="paragraph-9" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__regular__qJJtA text-module__small__sph8i body-module__full_width__kCIGb body-module__small_body__gOmDf article-body-module__paragraph__Ts-yF">In this White ⁠House, Trump serves as messenger-in-chief. These days, his less-scripted moments are revealing a president who feels increasingly boxed in by a Congress failing to deliver his <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-tells-republicans-pass-voting-law-for-jesus-2026-03-23/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">legislative</a> <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/legal/government/us-senate-clears-way-house-pass-funding-bill-end-dhs-shutdown-2026-04-02/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">priorities</a>, skeptical federal courts, <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/business/aerospace-defense/trump-threatens-nato-exit-scaling-up-tensions-with-allies-2026-04-01/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">reluctant foreign allies</a> and a <a data-testid="Link" referrerPolicy="no-referrer-when-downgrade" href="https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/hopes-dim-swift-end-iran-war-after-trump-speech-oil-prices-surge-anew-2026-04-02/" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__inherit-color__PhuPF text-module__inherit-font__1P1hv text-module__inherit-size__EyiQW link-module__link__INqxZ link-module__underline_default__-okuC">war in Iran</a> that’s complicated his push to rein in inflation.</div><h2 data-testid="Heading" class="text-module__text__0GDob text-module__dark-grey__UFC18 text-module__medium__2Rl30 text-module__heading_6__u1KdJ heading-module__base__p-zaD Hunnicutt is White House Correspondent at Reuters News. He writes about U.S. foreign and domestic policy and regularly travels with the President of the United States. Prior to joining the White House team in 2021, he covered presidential campaigns, economics, finance and investing for many years. He has also served on the board of the White House Correspondents' Association. Hunnicutt holds a bachelor's degree from Pomona College and a master's from the London School of Economics.</p><div class="author-bio-module__social__WLpHF" data-testid="SocialIcon"><ul Trump’s <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-tell-wary-public-that-iran-war-goals-have-been-accomplished-prime-time-2026-04-01/\">speech to Americans</a> on Wednesday told us little new about a month-old war that’s come to dominate his presidency. But the U.S. president’s comments this week to allies and even reporters about the limits on his power at home and abroad have been more revealing.","type":"paragraph"},"content_elements":[{"content":"Donald Trump’s <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-tell-wary-public-that-iran-war-goals-have-been-accomplished-prime-time-2026-04-01/\">speech to Americans</a> on Wednesday told us little new about a month-old war that’s come to dominate his presidency. But the U.S. president’s comments this week to allies and even reporters about the limits on his power at home and abroad have been more revealing.","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"Over the weekend, demonstrators opposed to Trump’s policies <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/us/rallies-planned-thousands-us-cities-no-kings-protest-against-trump-2026-03-28/\">gathered ​in thousands of U.S. cities</a> for protests held under the banner of “No Kings.”","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"\"They call me king now, do you believe it? ‘No king,’” Trump said ‌at the White House on Wednesday. \"I'm such a king I can't get <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/us-judge-halts-trumps-400-million-white-house-ballroom-project-now-2026-03-31/\">a ballroom</a> approved… I could be doing a lot more if I was a king.”","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"Candid, lively and discursive, those were Trump’s unfiltered remarks to close allies gathered for an Easter lunch. In the remarks, a powerful president who often projects confidence reflected on the restraints he faces. The comments came after Trump visited the Supreme Court in a ​historic first for a president and heard justices he appointed skeptically question a lawyer defending <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/legal/government/us-supreme-court-considers-trumps-effort-limit-birthright-citizenship-2026-04-01/\">his limits on birthright citizenship</a>. (Trump was accompanied to the court by Attorney ​General Pam Bondi, who he fired on Thursday)","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"Trump also suggested the financial demands of pursuing the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran were forcing ⁠hard choices at home. The federal government could not, he said, focus on issues like the cost of childcare. “We can't take care of daycare,\" Trump said. \"We have to take ​care of one thing: military protection. We have to guard the country.\"","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"Those comments were not what the public heard from the president on Wednesday. The event was closed to ​me and the other members of the press pool covering Trump that day and only became public when the White House briefly posted a video of the remarks online - and then deleted them shortly thereafter.","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"What the public saw instead Wednesday night was a different Trump: serious, defiant and tightly scripted, <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/us/takeaways-trumps-speech-iran-2026-04-02/\">delivering a primetime address</a> that did little to assuage fears that the Middle East conflict could widen.","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"The contrast was ​stark. When <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/us-leave-iran-pretty-quickly-return-if-needed-trump-tells-reuters-2026-04-01/\">my colleague Steve Holland</a> called Trump before the speech, the president vented about NATO in aggrieved language that never appeared in the formal address.","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"As reporters who follow Trump, ​we track the president’s moods and rhetorical styles closely. Teleprompter Trump is different from the looser telephone Trump, and both are different from the president at a rally or among his closest ‌confidants.","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"In this White ⁠House, Trump serves as messenger-in-chief. These days, his less-scripted moments are revealing a president who feels increasingly boxed in by a Congress failing to deliver his <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-tells-republicans-pass-voting-law-for-jesus-2026-03-23/\">legislative</a> <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/legal/government/us-senate-clears-way-house-pass-funding-bill-end-dhs-shutdown-2026-04-02/\">priorities</a>, skeptical federal courts, <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/business/aerospace-defense/trump-threatens-nato-exit-scaling-up-tensions-with-allies-2026-04-01/\">reluctant foreign allies</a> and a <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/hopes-dim-swift-end-iran-war-after-trump-speech-oil-prices-surge-anew-2026-04-02/\">war in Iran</a> that’s complicated his push to rein in inflation.","type":"paragraph"},{"level":1,"content":"CHART OF THE WEEK","type":"header"},{"id":"6BHW4TS4ZJKWNIV77XOM7QGFYQ","type":"graphic","url":"https://www.reuters.com/graphics/USA-POLLING/lbpgyjgywpq/chart.png","title":"Two-thirds of Americans want quick end to Iran war even if goals unachieved","description":"Stacked bar chart about public opinion on Iran war","byline":"Reuters Staff","graphic_type":"image","resizer_url":"https://www.reuters.com/resizer/v2/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.reuters.com%2Fgraphics%2FUSA-POLLING%2Flbpgyjgywpq%2Fchart.png?auth=46cc4e73243178eca19298c53598771eab182855438adee2f14a951d0fe4efd0","preview_image":{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/graphics/USA-POLLING/lbpgyjgywpq/chart.png"}},{"level":1,"content":"THE VIEW FROM BRUSSELS","type":"header"},{"content":"Trump’s primetime speech did not include direct criticism of NATO. But he has spent days <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/business/aerospace-defense/trump-threatens-nato-exit-scaling-up-tensions-with-allies-2026-04-01/\">bashing the military alliance</a> and <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/business/aerospace-defense/trump-says-us-strongly-considering-nato-exit-telegraph-newspaper-says-2026-04-01/\">even threatening to withdraw</a>, blasting its members as doing little to help with Iran and in the Strait of Hormuz. \"Hopefully, we're never going ​to need 'em,” Trump said on Wednesday. “I don't ​think we will need 'em.\"","type":"paragraph"},{"content":"Experts say it is <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/business/aerospace-defense/can-trump-pull-us-out-nato-2026-04-01/\">not ⁠clear</a> whether Trump could act unilaterally to leave the 77-year-old trans-Atlantic coalition, but Trump’s criticisms have troubled Washington’s major European allies. They’re pressing their case directly with the U.S. president: <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/finnish-president-tells-trump-more-european-nato-taking-shape-2026-04-01/\">Finnish President Alexander Stubb</a> spoke to Trump on Wednesday, and <a href=\"https://www.reuters.com/world/natos-rutte-visit-washington-next-week-long-planned-visit-2026-04-01/\">NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte</a> will visit ​Washington next week. It remains to be seen whether they can save an alliance that may be in tatters once the ​Iran war is over.","type":"paragraph"},{"level":1,"content":"TOP ⁠US POLITICS Rachel Faber reports.","duration":82.46,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"A judge’s order to halt construction on a new White House ballroom is drawing sharp pushback from the Trump administration,  Which says the ruling puts presidential security at risk. In an emergency motion filed Friday with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia,  The administration argued that the pause leaves the executive mansion 'open and exposed,' warning of what it calls grave national‑security harms. U.S. District Judge Richard Leon earlier this week ordered construction to stop while a lawsuit challenging the project moves forward. The lawsuit seeks to block the roughly $400 million ballroom planned for the site of the recently demolished East Wing,  Arguing the project required congressional approval. Leon temporarily delayed enforcement of his order for 14 days to allow the Trump administration to appeal. In its filing, the administration says the court lacks constitutional authority to hear the case,  As it 'rests on a single pedestrian’s subjective architectural feelings.' The initial lawsuit was brought by the National Trust for Historic Preservation,  A nonprofit that claims Trump exceeded his authority by razing the historic East Wing. The administration says the claims are legally baseless, arguing the president has complete authority to renovate the White House."},"published_time":"2026-04-04T22:23:04Z","updated_time":"2026-04-04T22:23:04Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d18eb3e4b0b60af064a631","caption":"A judge's order to stop construction work on a White House ballroom poses security risks, the Trump administration argued in an emergency motion that seeks to set aside the ruling. Ryan Brooks reports.","duration":125.2,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"Iranian forces on Saturday were hunting for a missing U.S. pilot whose warplane went down over Iran the day before. These images were released Friday by Iranian state media, and are said to show fragments of the jet. Officials say it was an F-15E fighter plane like this that was downed, and that two crew members were ejected but one remained missing. Both Washington and Tehran said the plane was hit by fire from a new Iranian air defense system. A state TV broadcast featuring a spokesperson for Iran's Revolutionary Guards said the new system also shot down three U.S. drones and two cruise missiles. Two U.S. officials told Reuters that two Black Hawk helicopters looking for the downed plane were hit by Iranian fire but made it out of Iranian airspace. The scale of that crew’s injuries was unclear, but it highlights the risk that remains for U.S. and Israeli craft over Iran,  Despite remarks by U.S. President Donald Trump and his Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth that U.S. forces had total control of the skies. The prospect of a U.S. service member on the run raises the stakes for Washington as the war enters its sixth week. We're going to bring them back to the Stone Ages where they belong. Trump has sent mixed messages since the conflict began, moving between open hostility and hinting at diplomatic progress aimed at ending the war,  Which has killed thousands, set off an energy crisis, and threatened lingering damage to the world economy. A recent Reuters/Ipsos poll showed low public support for the war. On Saturday Trump repeated a threat to intensify attacks on Iran if it failed to reach a deal or open the key Strait of Hormuz.  Writing in a social media post he said,  A post from Iran's foreign minister left the door open for peace talks,  But gave little sign of giving in to U.S. demands, saying Tehran cared about 'terms of a conclusive and lasting end to the illegal war that is imposed on us.'"},"published_time":"2026-04-04T20:22:57Z","updated_time":"2026-04-04T20:44:50Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d17269e4b01a8b78ae80ee-1775335318065","caption":"Iranian and U.S. forces were searching for a missing American pilot on Saturday from a warplane that was downed over Iran, while President Donald Trump warned Tehran time was running out on his latest deadline for a deal to end the war. Lucy Fielder has more.","duration":84.3,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"Ex-prisoners walk free, hours after the communist-led Cuban government announced a sweeping amnesty on Friday. It has coincided with the most intense pressure campaign applied by Washington in decades. The U.S. said it was closely monitoring whether those it deems to be political prisoners would be among those freed. That has long been a major sticking point in negotiations between Washington and Havana. Reuters spoke with several who had been pardoned of common crimes ranging from theft to bribery, but none had been involved in anti-government protests. 'I’m thankful for this gesture, 'says Harold Baez,' it gives me the chance to rebuild my life and rejoin society.' More than 2,000 prisoners are set to be released as part of what Cuba's state-run media has called a 'sovereign and humanitarian' gesture, the largest such amnesty in 10 years. The release of all, or the majority, of political prisoners would be seen as a major concession on Cuba's part in ongoing talks between the two countries. Human rights groups, some financed by the U.S. government, say Cuba is holding hundreds of political prisoners, with estimates varying, and promised scrutiny of the prisoner release process. Cuba has repeatedly denied it holds political prisoners. Authorities say those jailed during anti-government protests are found guilty of crimes including public disorder, resisting arrest, robbery and vandalism."},"published_time":"2026-04-04T12:57:10Z","updated_time":"2026-04-04T12:57:12Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d10a14e4b0b60af064a57b","caption":"Cuba began releasing prisoners from its jails on Friday just hours after its communist-run government announced a sweeping amnesty coinciding with the most intense pressure campaign applied by the U.S. in decades. The Earth up close, and then by the time we had a bit of a nap and got up, the Earth was just so far away again. And then to come in for that transitor injection, we came all the way back to Earth again. We were out there at 60,000 kilometres. We came back to within 200 kilometres of the planet. And it just felt like we were falling out of the sky, back to Earth, and I said to Reid, it feels like we're gonna hit it. It's amazing that we're actually gonna go around and miss this thing. It was just so close. And so to take all of that in was really phenomenal. We took some pictures earlier today, and then after putting them on the computer to look closer, we found a feature, you know, the 'Grand Canyon' of the Moon, it's called Orientale Basin, and we were able to see the entire thing. Standby… We were able to see the entire thing. And so it's clear that we are not on Earth, because that feature is not all visible from Earth. And so it's already special, the view that we have. And yes, the Earth is quite small and the moon is definitely getting bigger."},"published_time":"2026-04-04T09:32:50Z","updated_time":"2026-04-04T09:32:52Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d0da4de4b0b60af064a547","caption":"Astronauts aboard NASA's Artemis II Orion capsule early on Saturday (April 4) said the moon is \"definitely getting bigger,\" marking the third day of their high-stakes mission around the lunar surface, with the aim of returning humans to the moon later this decade.","type":"image","resizer_url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d0da4de4b0b60af064a547"},"source":{"hls":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/v3/playlist/759662/master.m3u8"},"channels":[{"id":"13978","name":"US News","regions":["US"]},{"id":"science","name":"Science ","regions":["US"]},{"id":"space","name":"Space","regions":["US","UK","IN"]},{"id":"World_News_US","name":"World News","regions":["US"]}],"advert_info":{"advert_params":[{"key":"adfriendly","value":true}],"advert_zone":"/4735792/reuters.com"},"meta_data":[{"key":"link","value":"tag:reuters.com,2026:newsml_RW988604042026RP1:4"},{"key":"byline","value":"IN SPACE"},{"key":"hidden","value":"false"},{"key":"region","value":"Remote"},{"key":"dateline","value":"Reuters, Reuters, APR 04"},{"key":"lmp-feed","value":"lp"},{"key":"copyright","value":"(c) 2026 Thomson Reuters, unless otherwise identified. Olivia Zollino reports.","duration":87.7,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday proposed to begin privatizing airport security operations handled by the Transportation Security Administration, in a bid to cut costs. The White House budget proposes cutting funding for the federal agency by $52 million and would require small airports to enroll in a program in which TSA pays for private screeners. It comes as Trump looks to cut 10 percent in non-defense spending for the 2027 fiscal year while proposing a massive $500 billion increase in the military budget as the U.S. continues its war against Iran. The request ultimately requires approval by Congress. TSA has about 50,000 federal employees who handle screening at nearly all U.S. airports. Budget documents released on Friday said airports currently using the privatization program have demonstrated savings compared to federal screening operations. Major US airports suffered massive disruptions after TSA security officers went unpaid since mid-February amid a budget dispute in Congress. More than 500 TSA officers have quit in recent weeks. Privatization could help remove TSA from congressional funding fights. But cuts to the airport security agency would come at a critical time for air travel, with rising concerns about air safety. Trump's budget also included money to hire more air traffic controllers."},"published_time":"2026-04-03T20:56:41Z","updated_time":"2026-04-03T20:56:43Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d028e8e4b01a8b78ae806f","caption":"President Donald Trump on Friday (April 3) proposed to begin privatizing airport security operations handled by the Transportation Security Administration, in an effort to save money. This report produced by Jillian Kitchener.","duration":74.46,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"These images, said to show fragments of a US fighter jet shot down over Iran, were released by Iranian State Media on Friday. A U.S. official told Reuters that search-and-rescue efforts have so far rescued one of the two crew members who ejected from the F-15 fighter jet. It is the first known incident of its kind in the nearly five-week-old war.  The Pentagon and U.S. Central Command did not respond to requests for comment. The prospect of U.S. pilots being alive and on the run inside Iran raises the stakes for the United States. An Iranian state news presenter announced a reward to citizens who capture and hand over enemy pilots alive to Iranian authorities. The presenter did not elaborate on the reward. Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps said it was combing an area near where the plane came down in Southwestern Iran. The incident follows threats by U.S. President Donald Trump to bomb the country back to the 'Stone Age,' including to attack Iran's energy infrastructure and desalination plants as he presses Tehran to end the war on U.S. terms."},"published_time":"2026-04-03T19:48:34Z","updated_time":"2026-04-03T19:48:35Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69d01903e4b01a8b78ae8063","caption":"A U.S. fighter jet has been shot down over Iran and search-and-rescue efforts have so far recovered one of two crew members who ejected, a U.S. official told Reuters, in the first known incident of its kind in the nearly five-week-old war. Olivia Zollino reports.","duration":60.9,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"U.S. job growth rebounded sharply in March, with non-farm payrolls increasing by 178,000, marking the biggest increase in 15 months. It followed a sharp decline in February, but the rebound is overstating the labor market's health. The healthcare sector accounted for most of the job gains, adding 76,000 positions, though around half were from employees returning to work at physicians' offices following a strike. Employment also increased at hospitals. And warmer weather gave a boost to construction employment, which increased by 26,000 positions. Economists said it is too early to capture the fallout from the Middle East conflict, with some are expecting that it could become evident by next month. The national average retail gasoline price this week topped $4 a gallon for the first time in more than three years. This will feed through to higher inflation and erode households' purchasing power, offsetting some of the strength in wage growth and slowing spending."},"published_time":"2026-04-03T18:01:25Z","updated_time":"2026-04-03T18:01:28Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69cfffece4b0b60af064a4f4","caption":"U.S. job growth rebounded sharply in March as a strike by healthcare workers ended and temperatures warmed up, but downside risks for the labor market are mounting from ​a war with Iran that has no clear end in sight. Urman described her legacy as \"she tried to please the boss, but it was never enough.\"","duration":79.73,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"\"He didn't like the kind of mess of the Epstein files. He was upset about that. And then also a lot prosecutions of his enemies were getting nullified by judges.\"  \"There really historically was supposed to be some  independence between the Department of Justice and the White House. And, you know, I think she politicized it to a degree that we hadn't seen before.  So, I think her legacy would be she tried to please the boss, but it was never enough.\"  “It's hard to imagine someone, you know, being more loyal than Pam Bondi, although Todd Blanche probably fits the mould as well as anyone because,  you know, he's on social media a lot,  you know, big talking up the DOJ. This report produced by Freddie Joyner.","duration":87.36,"transcript_data":{"transcript":"'I will fight every day.' President Donald Trump on Thursday removed Pam Bondi from her post as U.S. Attorney General. The move follows mounting frustration with her performance, including her handling of files related to Jeffrey Epstein, which drew renewed scrutiny of Trump's past friendship with the late financier and sex offender. 'You don't tell me anything, you washed up loser lawyer..you're not even a lawyer.' During a combative hearing before a House of Representatives panel earlier this year, Bondi responded to criticism with political attacks directed at lawmakers. She also refused to apologize or look at Epstein victims and their relatives who attended the proceedings. Trump had also reportedly grown frustrated that Bondi was not moving quickly enough to prosecute his critics and adversaries. 'These were corrupt, radical left Democrats, because Comey essentially was a—he's worse than a Democrat.' The targets included former FBI Director James Comey and New York Attorney General Letitia James, neither of whom faced criminal charges after investigations, with both cases ultimately dismissed. Bondi's ouster could lead to a shake-up in strategy at the Justice Department and potentially a renewed push to deploy the U.S. legal system against Trump’s enemies. In the interim, Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, Trump’s former personal lawyer, will lead the Justice Department."},"published_time":"2026-04-02T20:37:41Z","updated_time":"2026-04-02T20:37:42Z","thumbnail":{"url":"https://ajo.prod.reuters.tv/api/v2/img/69ced303e4b01a8b78ae7ec5","caption":"U.S. President Donald Trump has removed Attorney General Pam Bondi from her post, a White House official said on Thursday, following mounting frustration with her performance, including her handling of investigative files related to the late financier and sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
